evals.query: In my Kora service I need a job that runs every day at 3 AM to generate a nightly report. How do I schedule it?
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:scheduling-quartz dependency and the mandatory annotation processor (annotation-processors for Java or symbol-processors for Kotlin)
Plugs QuartzModule into the @KoraApp interface
Annotates a method on a @Component class with @ScheduleWithCron("0 0 3 * * ?") imported from ru.tinkoff.kora.scheduling.quartz
Places the scheduled method on a @Component class (which may remain final in Java)
Does not use field injection or a non-Kora @Scheduled-style annotation

evals.query: I want the cron schedule of my Quartz job to be configurable in application.conf instead of hardcoded in the annotation.
expected_behavior: Uses @ScheduleWithCron(config = "job") and points config at a HOCON node
Defines job { cron = "..." } in application.conf
Explains that the config value takes priority over the annotation value
Does not invent a non-existent annotation attribute

evals.query: I need a Quartz job that fires every 5 seconds using a custom Trigger, not a cron expression, in my Kora app.
expected_behavior: Declares an org.quartz.Trigger built with TriggerBuilder and SimpleScheduleBuilder as a tagged component on the @KoraApp interface using @Tag
Annotates the job method with @ScheduleWithTrigger(@Tag(SomeClass.class)) matching the Trigger's tag
Notes that @ScheduleWithTrigger takes a @Tag reference, not a string name
Does not use field injection or a non-Kora scheduling annotation

evals.query: My hourly Quartz job sometimes runs longer than an hour and I get two overlapping executions. How do I prevent that in Kora?
expected_behavior: Adds @DisallowConcurrentExecution alongside @ScheduleWithCron from ru.tinkoff.kora.scheduling.quartz
Explains that this prevents the same job from running in parallel
Optionally mentions scheduling.waitForJobComplete for graceful shutdown
Does not suggest a manual lock as the primary solution instead of the built-in annotation

evals.query: I just need to run a cleanup task every 30 seconds in my Kora service. Should I use Quartz for that?
expected_behavior: Recommends the lighter scheduling-jdk module with @ScheduleAtFixedRate or @ScheduleWithFixedDelay for plain fixed intervals
Explains that Quartz is for cron expressions or custom Triggers, not simple timers
Does not pull in scheduling-quartz unnecessarily
Does not reference a non-Kora @Scheduled annotation

evals.query: I want my Quartz jobs in Kora to survive restarts and run across a cluster using PostgreSQL for storage.
expected_behavior: Configures the JDBC JobStore via org.quartz.jobStore.class = org.quartz.impl.jdbcjobstore.JobStoreTX in the quartz config block
Sets a driverDelegateClass (e.g. PostgreSQLDelegate), a dataSource, and clustering with isClustered and instanceId = AUTO
Mentions creating the Quartz schema tables in the database
Pairs stateful jobs with @PersistJobDataAfterExecution and @DisallowConcurrentExecution
Does not claim RAMJobStore (the default) persists across restarts

evals.query: My long-running Quartz batch job must stop cleanly when the Kora service receives SIGTERM. How do I handle graceful shutdown?
expected_behavior: Sets scheduling.waitForJobComplete to control whether shutdown waits for running jobs
Checks Thread.currentThread().isInterrupted() inside the job loop and returns early
Explains the interrupt is delivered to the running job thread on shutdown
Does not rely on non-Kora lifecycle hooks
